response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a disciplined full-stack web engineer with genuine geospatial side-project experience (GISCollective, Green Map), but the role's core requirement of modern C++ or Rust is essentially unmet — his only C++ exposure was a single six-month contract in 2014-2015, and Rust is absent from his profile entirely. His primary commercial stack is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang, which does not transfer to the low-level systems work demanded by a routing tile pipeline. While he demonstrates distributed-systems intuition from his Sociomantic tenure and a strong engineering culture via TDD advocacy, there is no routing, navigation, or tile-generation domain experience, making him a poor fit for this mid-senior C++ systems role.
strengths: Genuine geospatial/GIS background through GISCollective and Green Map projects, including a FOSS4G conference presentation
High-throughput distributed-systems exposure at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O and low-latency design
Strong engineering discipline evidenced by consistent TDD advocacy, open-source testing tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), and community teaching
concerns: C++ experience is limited to a single six-month role in 2014-2015; Rust is entirely absent — both are the primary required languages for this position
No routing, navigation, or tile-generation domain experience; geospatial work is confined to hobbyist and NGO mapping applications rather than production routing pipelines

Career trajectory is firmly in web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) with no trend toward systems programming, making a senior-level pivot to C++ routing infrastructure unlikely to succeed
